title: Quadratic Equations
subject: math
audioScript: This chapter covers quadratic equations in standard form a x squared plus b x plus c equals zero. Solutions can be found by factorization or the quadratic formula. The discriminant b squared minus four a c tells us whether roots are real and distinct, equal, or not real. Always check whether the equation can be simplified before applying the formula.

sections.title: 1. Concept Overview
sections.body: A quadratic equation in one variable has the form ax² + bx + c = 0 where a ≠ 0. CBSE Class 10 expects factorization for simple cases and the quadratic formula for general cases, plus interpretation of the discriminant.
sections.visualSvg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 360 200' width='100%' height='200'><path d='M20 160 Q90 20 180 160 T340 160' fill='none' stroke='#22d3ee' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='160' x2='340' y2='160' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='180' y1='20' x2='180' y2='180' stroke='#64748b' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='4'/><text x='180' y='195' text-anchor='middle' fill='#94a3b8' font-size='11'>Parabola y = ax² + bx + c</text></svg>

sections.title: 4. Formulas / Theorems
sections.body:
- Standard form: **ax² + bx + c = 0**
- Quadratic formula: **x = (−b ± √(b² − 4ac)) / 2a**
- Discriminant: **D = b² − 4ac**
- If D > 0: two distinct real roots; D = 0: equal roots; D < 0: no real roots

sections.title: 5. Solved Examples
sections.body:
**Example:** Solve x² − 5x + 6 = 0

Factor: (x − 2)(x − 3) = 0 → x = 2 or x = 3

**Check:** Substitute back into the original equation.

sections.title: 12. Teacher Explanation Module
sections.body: Before reaching for the formula, ask: can I split the middle term or factor by grouping? The discriminant is not magic — it only reports what factorization already hinted at. Use a number line sketch when students struggle with the ± sign.
